assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable/payable is denominated in a foreign currency and is a monetary item between group entities.
The related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of the FX risk on the intragroup monetary receivable
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is a genuine hedge designation route because IFRS 9 allows a fair value hedge of a recognised asset for a specific risk component, and the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item can qualify in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of the FX risk on the intragroup monetary receivable
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is also a genuine hedge designation route because IFRS 9 recognises cash flow hedges for a recognised asset or liability for a particular risk, and the intragroup FX exposure can qualify in consolidation when it affects consolidated profit or loss.
